items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/` **Triggered by:** PR #58396 by `@RaviPidaparthi` ## What Changed PR #58396 added the following to `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` (released as `1.0.0-beta.23`): - **New public type `PlatformHeaders`** ŌĆö a static class centralizing all platform HTTP header name constants (`x-request-id`, `x-platform-server`, `x-agent-session-id`, isolation keys, `traceparent`, `x-ms-client-request-id`) - **`RequestIdMiddleware`** ŌĆö sets the `x-request-id` response header automatically on every HTTP response (OTEL trace ID ŌåÆ incoming header ŌåÆ GUID) - **`RequestIdBaggagePropagator`** ŌĆö propagates `x-request-id` into `Activity.Baggage` for distributed tracing correlation - **Simplified Tier 3 API** ŌĆö replaced 6 individual `Add/Use` extension methods with a single `AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()` pair - **Breaking change**: Removed `IsolationContext.UserIsolationKeyHeaderName` and `IsolationContext.ChatIsolationKeyHeaderName`; use `PlatformHeaders.UserIsolationKey` and `PlatformHeaders.ChatIsolationKey` instead ## Gaps Found - `PlatformHeaders` is a **new public API** but is absent from the README Key Concepts section ŌĆö Tier 3 users and protocol-package authors who reference header name constants will not discover it from the README - The `x-request-id` automatic correlation behavior (RequestIdMiddleware sets it on every response using OTEL trace ID ŌåÆ incoming header ŌåÆ GUID fallback) is undocumented beyond a one-line changelog entry; users debugging distributed traces need this explained - The README Key Concepts section still lists `AddAgentServerVersion()` / `AddAgentServerLogging()` in the Logging paragraph but does not explain the new unified `AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()` pair, which replaces six calls for Tier 3 setups - No snippet or example shows how to read or use `PlatformHeaders` constants in custom middleware or Tier 3 setups - The `samples/Sample2_Configuration.md` sample file does not mention `PlatformHeaders` or the `x-request-id` correlation pattern
­¤ōÉ Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md`** ŌĆö Edit Add a new **`PlatformHeaders`** entry to the Key Concepts section (after the `Health endpoint` subsection and before the `## Examples` section): ```markdown ### PlatformHeaders `PlatformHeaders` is a static class that centralises all HTTP header name constants used across the AgentServer platform. Protocol packages and custom middleware should reference these constants rather than hard-coding header name strings. | Constant | Header | Direction | |---|---|---| | `PlatformHeaders.RequestId` | `x-request-id` | Request (client correlation) / Response (always set) | | `PlatformHeaders.ServerVersion` | `x-platform-server` | Response (server identity) | | `PlatformHeaders.SessionId` | `x-agent-session-id` | Response (resolved session) | | `PlatformHeaders.UserIsolationKey` | `x-agent-user-isolation-key` | Request (platform-injected) | | `PlatformHeaders.ChatIsolationKey` | `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` | Request (platform-injected) | | `PlatformHeaders.TraceParent` | `traceparent` | Request (W3C Trace Context) | | `PlatformHeaders.ClientRequestId` | `x-ms-client-request-id` | Request (Azure SDK correlation) | The `x-request-id` header is automatically set on every response by `RequestIdMiddleware` (included in both `AgentHostBuilder` and the `AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()` Tier 3 setup). The value is resolved as: OTEL trace ID ŌåÆ incoming `x-request-id` header ŌåÆ new GUID. Downstream code can read the resolved value from `HttpContext.Items[PlatformHeaders.RequestIdItemKey]`. ``` Also, update the existing **Logging** paragraph in Key Concepts to clarify `AddAgentServerCore()`: Find and replace this sentence: ``` for Tier 3, call `AddAgentServerCore()` and `UseAgentServerCore()` to enable it. ``` with: ``` for Tier 3 (standalone ASP.NET Core hosts), call `AddAgentServerCore()` and `UseAgentServerCore()`. These two calls replace the six individual extension methods that existed in earlier beta versions and register request-ID correlation, version headers, and inbound request logging in one step. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets Add a new snippet demonstrating `PlatformHeaders` usage in a Tier 3 (standalone) setup. **Test file:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/tests/Samples/Sample3_PlatformHeaders.cs` Create this file: ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core; using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder; using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core.Tests.Samples; public class Sample3_PlatformHeaders { [Test] [Ignore("Illustrative sample ŌĆö not a runnable test")] public void PlatformHeaders_Tier3Setup() { #region Snippet:Core_ReadMe_PlatformHeaders var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(); // AddAgentServerCore registers RequestIdMiddleware, server-version header, and inbound logging. builder.Services.AddAgentServerCore(); var app = builder.Build(); app.UseAgentServerCore(); // Read the resolved request-correlation ID from HttpContext.Items in your own middleware. app.Use(async (context, next) => { if (context.Items.TryGetValue(PlatformHeaders.RequestIdItemKey, out var requestId)) { // requestId matches the x-request-id response header for this request. context.Response.Headers[PlatformHeaders.RequestId] = requestId?.ToString(); } await next(context); }); #endregion } } ``` Add a snippet placeholder in `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` inside the `## Examples` section: ```markdown ### Using PlatformHeaders constants in a Tier 3 setup ```C# Snippet:Core_ReadMe_PlatformHeaders ``` ``` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` currently contains all required sections. The changes above are additions within existing sections, so no sections need to be created or reordered. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` ŌĆö injects snippet content from the new test file into the README placeholder 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` ŌĆö no new public types were added, but run to confirm the API listing is current 5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [AgentServer] Docs: Document PlatformHeaders and x-request-id correlation in Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core README
